title: mums2
text: even begin process analyzing data, preprocess . import process data readable data.frame object can view transform. process data need run two functions: import_all_data(), ms2_ms1_compare(). import_all_data() creates object reflecting imported data, ms2_ms1_compare() assigns ms2 spectra ms1 data. However, data noise, may need transformed can properly analysis . accommodate issues, two functions: filter_peak_table(), change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute(). filter_peak_table() allows remove low-quality features change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute() allows transform retention minutes seconds. allows ensure retention time matches ms2 data retention time. explain detail function go pipeline.

title: mums2
text: import_all_data() function takes peak_table meta_data table input converts mpactr object (wrapper mpactr::import_data()). format parameter accepts three different formats: Metaboscape, Progenesis, None. using feature table exported metaboscape, use “Metaboscape” format. Progenesis, use “Progenesis” format. order “Metaboscape” “Progenesis” work, mpactr transforms data behind scenes universal peak table. “None” format used using “universal peak table” “peak table” parameter. can read formats (mpactr::import_data()). need deeper understanding format peak table meta data file show , look mpactr’s getting started page Meta data csv provides information samples. order meta data valid, needs following columns: “Injection”, “Sample_Code”, ” Biological_Group.” “Injection” column name samples, match samples inside peak_table. “Sample_Code” id technical replicates. Finally, “Biological_Group” another id, biological replicate groups (can learn mpactr::import_data()).
code: data <- import_all_data(   peak_table = mums2::mums2_example("full_mix_peak_table.csv"),    meta_data = mums2::mums2_example("full_mix_meta_data.csv"),    format = "None") #> If peak table has corrupted compound names they will be converted to #>       utf-8 and if there are any commas, they will be converted to periods(.).

Therefore, depending big file , might take moments function complete. ms2_ms1_compare() generates list data two data.frames (“ms1_data”, “peak_data”), list (“peak_data”), character vector (“samples”). ms2_matches - One two data.frames, “ms2_matches”, data.frame contains five columns: “mz”, “rt”, “ms1_compound_id”, “spectra_index”, “ms2_spectrum_id.” “mz” “rt”, represent ms2 mass charge ratio retention time. “ms1_compound_id” represents ms1 compound id imported feature table. “spectra_index” matches ms2 data ms2 spectrum. Finally, “ms2_spectrum_id” similar “ms1_compound_id”, generated name represent ms2 spectra (name combination mz rt). necessary properly compare compounds. Since information supplied MS2 file compute similar results compounds alike, can use algorithmic techniques compute similarity two compounds. allows us generate annotations cluster similar spectra (MS2 matched information) together. ms1_data - “data.frame”, “ms1_data”, data.frame created mpactr object. peak_data - list generate ms2_ms1_compare() named “peak_data.” “peak_data” collection ms2 peak list. peak list collection fragment ions, value represent intensity mass-charge ratio. samples - last slot inside list character vector named “samples.” just list groups/samples contained inside peak_table file.

title: create community matrix — create_community_matrix
text: cluster_object result cluster_data() function. data <- import_all_data(peak_table = mums2::mums2_example("full_mix_peak_table_small.csv"), meta_data = mums2::mums2_example("full_mix_meta_data_small.csv"), format = "Metaboscape") filtered_data <- data |> filter_peak_table(filter_mispicked_ions_params()) |> filter_peak_table(filter_cv_params(cv_threshold = 0.2)) |> filter_peak_table(filter_group_params(group_threshold = 0.1, "Blanks")) |> filter_peak_table(filter_insource_ions_params()) change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute(filtered_data, "minutes") matched_data <- ms2_ms1_compare(mums2_example("full_mix_ms2_small.mgf"), filtered_data, 2, 6) dist <- dist_ms2(data = matched_data, cutoff = 0.3, precursor_thresh = 2, score_params = modified_cosine_params(0.5), min_peaks = 0) cluster_results <- cluster_data(distance_df = dist, ms2_match_data = matched_data, cutoff = 0.3, cluster_method = "opticlust") community_matrix <- create_community_matrix_object(cluster_results)
